characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: For all their dysfunction, they are a loving family. Louis and Ren in particular have many moments of this. Big, Screwed-Up Family: A lighter and sillier example, but they can have their moments of tension. Happily Married: Steve and Eileen. They have their issues (such as Steve's insecurities over Eileen's successful career and her "bringing home the bacon" more than him inThe Movie), but overall, they're solid and still deeply love each other. Informed Judaism: It's revealed in the episode "Heck of a Hanukkah" that they celebrate Jewish holidays, perhaps in reference to Eileen's actress, plus Louis's actor's mother, being Jewish. However, it's not a central part of their characters. Sibling Yin-Yang: All three of the Stevens children are this. Donnie is aDumb Jock, Ren is anAcademic Alpha Bitch, and Louis is a mischievousprankster.

characters.name: As A Whole
characters.description: Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: For all their dysfunction, they are a loving family. Louis and Ren in particular have many moments of this. Big, Screwed-Up Family: A lighter and sillier example, but they can have their moments of tension. Happily Married: Steve and Eileen. They have their issues (such as Steve's insecurities over Eileen's successful career and her "bringing home the bacon" more than him inThe Movie), but overall, they're solid and still deeply love each other. Informed Judaism: It's revealed in the episode "Heck of a Hanukkah" that they celebrate Jewish holidays, perhaps in reference to Eileen's actress, plus Louis's actor's mother, being Jewish. However, it's not a central part of their characters. Sibling Yin-Yang: All three of the Stevens children are this. Donnie is aDumb Jock, Ren is anAcademic Alpha Bitch, and Louis is a mischievousprankster.

characters.name: Louis
characters.description: The youngest Stevens child. Annoying Younger Sibling: To both Donnie and Ren, especially the latter, as their radically different personalities make them clash a lot more and Louis especially loves to pull pranks on her. The Baby of the Bunch: Being the youngest of his siblings makes him this among the rest of the family. Black Sheep: Unlike the athletically gifted Donnie and academically-minded Ren, Louis doesn't particularly excel at more "conventional" success paths. Book Dumb: Louis does poorly in school and gets detention regularly (to the point that he plans his after-school time around it). He's shown to be smarter than Donnie (although that's admittedly not really saying much) and his schemes require a good deal of cunning to plan and normally succeed.

characters.name: Ren
characters.description: The middle child and only daughter of the Stevens family. Academic Alpha Bitch: She's highly studious and takes her gradesveryseriously. Brainy Brunette: Has long brown hair and is very intelligent and studious. Even the Girls Want Her: A number of female students idolize her.Marla and Carlaare the most frequently seen. Extracurricular Enthusiast: Aside from her general academic performance, she also does these to look better for when she goes to a good college.

characters.name: Donnie
characters.description: The oldest Stevens sibling. Big Brother Bully: He occasionally has his moments of being one to Louis and even Ren on some occasions. Brainless Beauty: He's certainly a heartthrob, but his intelligence is nowhere near the level of his looks. Dumb Jock: He's good at sports, but is also an airhead. Dumb Muscle: He's great at sports, but is really not the brightest bulb.

characters.name: Steve
characters.description: A quirky lawyer and the patriarch of the Stevens family. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: In "Boy on a Rock", when Steve becomes a volunteer at school, Louis asks if he's going to embarrass him and Ren. Steve admits that it's inevitable. He ends up working in the cafeteria and puts on a show of holding a cowboy-themed barbecue. Like Father, Like Son: Like Donnie, he played football in his youth and also wrestled. Played Ball in College: He wrestled and played running back at Michigan State University. In "Hardly Famous", he's recognized as the legendary "Stiffy" Stevens by Barry Hudson Jr., a famous crooner-type singer and guest of the Stevens family. Hudson fawns over Steve, calling him a "legend" with "the best stiff arm in the game". Repetitive Name: Steve Stevens.

characters.name: Eileen
characters.description: The matriarch of the Stevens family. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: In "Gutter Queen", Eileen's repeated bowling fails (to the point of somehow getting a limb stuck in the ball return) make Ren mortified to be part of a mother/daughter bowling league.

characters.name: Twitty
characters.description: Chick Magnet: Twitty has so many girls ask him to the Sadie Hawkins dance that he puts their pictures up and picks by dart board. Last-Name Basis: Is almost always called by his last name. My God, What Have I Done?: InThe Movie, he is disgusted with himself for helping a reality prank TV show cast the Stevens family and put them through humiliating ordeals to make them into laughingstocks and is driven to get them out and pull the plug on it.

characters.name: Tawny
characters.description: Louis' best female friend and eventually girlfriend. Only Sane Woman: She usually serves as the voice of reason. Perky Goth: By Disney standards. She dresses in a darker fashion compared to her classmates and refuses to conform to others' standards, but is also nice and reasonable.

characters.name: Beans
characters.description: The Stevens' extremely annoying neighbor. Over time, he takes part in Louis and Twitty's schemes. Cousin Oliver: Parodied. He’s a cute kid that's brought in part way though the series to boost ratings but ends up being a nuisance to the main characters. Louis and Twitty do include him in their plans sometimes but only in situations they might find him useful. However, he's also somewhat of the opposite of this trope in that while he was initially brought in as a sort of ratings gimmick, he ended up being one of the most, if not the most, memorable characters of the show. The Thing That Would Not Leave: He's this for the Stevens family - he shows up at their house uninvited, makes himself at home, and refuses to leave. Trademark Favorite Food: Bacon. His love for it is aRunning Gag.

characters.name: Tom
characters.description: Louis's nerdy friend and classmate. Gadgeteer Genius: He likes to make all sorts of inventions as much as Louis does but, as an intellectually gifted youth, doesn't do so for pranks. Minored in Ass-Kicking: He's surprisingly skilled in martial arts.

characters.name: Principal Wexler
characters.description: The principal of Lawrence Jr. High School, who absolutely loves Ren for being an exemplary student and despises Louis for being the opposite. Dean Bitterman: He constantly clashes with Louis while adoring Ren. Of course, it doesn't help that Louis practically plans his afterschool time around his detention days.

characters.name: Coach Tugnut
characters.description: The physical education teacher at Lawrence Jr. High School, who once coached Donnie and continues to idolize him. Butt-Monkey: Frequently the target of Louis's pranks, much to his annoyance.
